title: AgentProvenance
description: Per-write provenance metadata recording which model(s) produced an agent contribution, when, and where (RRP-0025 + RRP-0026). Attaches to Author entries, Annotation.created_by, and SubmissionRequest.created_by. Multi-model: a single contribution can declare multiple models (e.g. Claude Opus 4.7 for prose + GPT-5 for code review) via the `models[]` array. Backward compat with RRP-0025: the flat fields (model_slug, model_family, model_release_date, context_window_tokens) at the root are deprecated aliases — consumers MUST prefer `models[0].*` when both are present.

models.description: Ordered list of models used for this contribution. Primary model first by convention (not enforced). For single-model agents (the common case), the array has one entry. See RRP-0026.

inference_started_at.description: Wall-clock UTC timestamp when the agent began the work.

inference_ended_at.description: Wall-clock UTC timestamp when the agent completed the work.

inference_wall_seconds.description: Total wall-clock seconds spent on this contribution.

inference_environment.description: Human-readable identifier for where the inference ran. Free-form. Examples: 'Claude Code CLI', 'claude.ai web', 'Anthropic API', 'OpenAI Playground', 'OpenRouter + Claude Code CLI' for multi-vendor setups.

operator_orcid.description: ORCID iD of the human operator who initiated this agent run, if known. Distinct from the agent's own identity — captures attribution back to a human-in-the-loop for accountability.

model_slug.description: DEPRECATED (RRP-0026): alias for `models[0].release_pin || models[0].name`. Consumers reading this field MUST prefer `models[0].*` when both are present. Writers SHOULD emit `models[]` and omit this field.

ModelDescriptor.description: One model used in producing the contribution. Only `name` is required; every other field is recommended-but-optional, so captures degrade gracefully when metadata is partial.

name.description: Full canonical marketing name (e.g. 'Claude Opus 4.7', 'GPT-5', 'Gemini 3 Pro', 'Llama 4 405B'). Vendor + series + version, fully spelled. This is the human-facing identifier — what appears in citations and search results. RRP-0026 §`Author.name` convention says agent authors SHOULD set Author.name to match models[0].name in the single-model case.

vendor.description: Vendor identifier, lowercase shortname (anthropic, openai, google, meta, mistral, deepseek, ...). Used for cohort aggregation on the vendor axis.

family.description: Family within a vendor (claude, gpt, gemini, llama, mistral, deepseek). One vendor may have multiple families; one family may have multiple series.

series.description: Model series within a family (opus, sonnet, haiku, gpt-5, gpt-4o, o3, gemini-pro, gemini-flash). Distinguishes capability tiers.

version.description: Marketing version string ('4.7', '5.0', '3.0-pro'). Free-form within the family/series convention; vendors are inconsistent here so the schema doesn't enforce a pattern.

release_pin.description: Exact API model identifier as exposed by the inference vendor (e.g. 'claude-opus-4-7-20260520', 'gpt-5-2026-04-15'). Lowercase, hyphens/dots/digits. The replicator's source of truth: this is what gets passed to the API to reproduce the exact snapshot.

release_date.description: Date the model snapshot was released by the vendor, YYYY-MM-DD. Typically encoded in the release_pin suffix.

context_window_tokens.description: Effective context window the agent was operating in.

inference_provider.description: Where the inference ran when distinct from `vendor` (e.g. 'openrouter', 'vertex-ai', 'azure-openai', 'aws-bedrock'). Omit if vendor and provider are the same.
